title: The Agent documents the open Northstar IOLTA trust reconciliation item for the May period - either by updating the open exception on account 105000 (IOLTA - Client Trust Account) with an identified root cause and a proposed resolution, or by adding a reconciliation review note against the May 105000 reconciliation (or similar) - so the previously undiagnosed item is now recorded.
justification: The prompt directs Devon to get "the open item documented the way it should have been from the start." The item was sitting with no root cause and no resolution, so the write action that records the diagnosis is required. Phrased method-agnostically because documenting via the exception record or a review note are both acceptable per the Oracle Events.
evidence: See OE 4, OE 11. Look for a successful update to the account 105000 exception (root cause and proposed resolution populated) or a new review note created against the May 105000 reconciliation. Confirm the tool response indicates success.

title: The Agent's documentation of the open item identifies the cause of the $12,460.00 variance on account 105000 as reclassification entry JE-northstar_legal-FP-2026-05-0055, posted June 9, 2026, debiting account 105000 (IOLTA - Client Trust Account) and crediting account 120000 (Client Cost Advances) for $12,460.00 (or similar wording).
justification: The prompt directs Devon to get "the open item documented the way it should have been from the start." Documentation that omits the cause-to-entry link does not meet that standard. The variance, the driving entry, its direction, and the two accounts come from one diagnosis and are graded together; the preparer and control-failure characterization is a distinct claim graded separately in the next rubric.
evidence: See OE 11. Inspect the root cause text on the exception or the review note body for the $12,460.00 figure and reclass entry JE-northstar_legal-FP-2026-05-0055 (DR 105000 / CR 120000) posted June 9, 2026. Look for these elements in the documentation record.

title: The Agent's documentation of the open item states that entry JE-northstar_legal-FP-2026-05-0055 was prepared, approved, and posted by the same person, Edith Banda, with no late-post authorization (or similar wording).
justification: The prompt directs Devon to get "the open item documented the way it should have been from the start," which includes recording why the controls failed. The self-review (one person prepared, approved, and posted) and the missing late-post authorization are a distinct control-failure claim, graded separately from the cause-to-entry link.
evidence: See OE 11. Inspect the root cause text on the exception or the review note body for Edith Banda named as preparer, approver, and poster of entry JE-northstar_legal-FP-2026-05-0055 with no late-post authorization. Look for these elements in the documentation record.

title: The Agent's documentation of the open item proposes reversing that reclassification entry so account 105000 ties back to the $2,466,828.36 client trust liability in account 250000 (or similar wording).
justification: Documentation that omits the proposed fix does not meet the prompt's standard of documenting the item properly. The proposed reversal and the $2,466,828.36 tie-out target are a distinct content claim from the cause diagnosis and are graded separately.
evidence: See OE 11, OE 12. Inspect the proposed resolution text on the exception or the review note body for the proposed reversal restoring account 105000 to $2,466,828.36 so trust cash agrees to the accont 250000 liability.

title: The Agent hands the proposed correcting entry to the close team to action, reaching Daniel Jones ([daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com](mailto:daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com)) and/or Edith Banda ([edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com](mailto:edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com)) by email or an equivalent direct message.
justification: The prompt says Devon will not post into the client ledger himself but wants the correction "worked out and handed to the close team to action." This confirms the handoff reached the correct close-team recipient(s). Phrased method-agnostically on channel because the prompt names the goal (hand it to the close team) without specifying a channel; the content is checked in Rubric 5.
evidence: See OE 10, OE 12. Look for a sent email or message addressed to daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com and/or edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com conveying the proposed correction. Confirm the tool response indicates success.

title: The Agent's handoff to the close team specifies the correcting entry as debit account 120000 (Client Cost Advances) / credit account 105000 (IOLTA - Client Trust Account) for $12,460.00, which restores account 105000 to $2,466,828.36 so trust cash equals the client trust liability, and asks the close team to post it on the client ledger (or similar wording).
justification: A handoff without the exact accounts, direction, amount, and the request to post does not let the close team action the fix. These values come from the same correcting entry and are graded together.
evidence: See OE 12. Inspect the handoff content for debit 120000 / credit 105000, the $12,460.00 amount, the resulting $2,466,828.36 tie-out, and an explicit request for the close team to post the entry.

title: The Agent does not post or reverse the correcting journal entry in the Northstar client ledger itself (it neither creates-and-posts a client journal entry nor posts a reversal of the reclass to northstar_legal).
justification: The prompt and Devon's authority are explicit that he will not post into the client's ledger; posting the correction himself is an authority breach and a task failure. Drafting an unposted proposed entry purely as documentation to hand off is acceptable; auto-posting it is not.
evidence: See OE 12 and the authority guardrails. Confirm there is no posted client journal-entry creation or posted reversal against northstar_legal for the $12,460.00 correction in the trajectory.

title: The Agent grants Mia Hartwell ([mia.hartwell@brookfieldcpas.com](mailto:mia.hartwell@brookfieldcpas.com)) viewer/read access to the restricted close-team workpaper "Northstar Legal - IOLTA Client Trust Reconciliation Workpaper - May 2026" (document doc_2b7f4c9a1e0d6358).
justification: The prompt says when Mia could not reach the close team's support, "get her sorted with the access she needs." Mia has no access grant to that restricted workpaper, so granting it is a required write action with a specific grantee and target document.
evidence: See OE 9, OE 13. Look for a successful access grant on document doc_2b7f4c9a1e0d6358 with grantee mia.hartwell@brookfieldcpas.com in a viewer/read role. Confirm the tool response indicates success.

title: The Agent delivers a review note / memo for Julia Vance ([julia.vance@brookfieldcpas.com](mailto:julia.vance@brookfieldcpas.com)) with the findings and a recommendation - by sending it to her or by capturing it as a restricted memo addressed to her review (or similar).
justification: The prompt asks Devon to "pull it together for Julia as a clean review note with a clear recommendation." This confirms the partner-facing deliverable reached Julia. Method-agnostic because an emailed note or a vault memo for her review are both acceptable per the Oracle Events.
evidence: See OE 10, OE 14. Look for a sent message to julia.vance@brookfieldcpas.com, or a restricted memo created for her review, containing the Northstar IOLTA findings. Confirm the tool response indicates success.

title: The Agent's review note to Julia Vance states the $12,460.00 IOLTA trust break on Northstar account 105000 (IOLTA client-trust cash) versus account 250000 (client trust liability) for May, with the cause attributed to the self-reviewed reclassification entry prepared, approved, and posted by Edith Banda (or similar).
justification: A "clean review note with a clear recommendation" must carry the finding and its cause. The break amount, the two accounts, and the self-review cause come from the same finding and are graded together as one content element.
evidence: See OE 14. Inspect the note/memo content for the $12,460.00 break (105000 vs 250000) and the self-review reclass cause attributed to Edith Banda.

title: The Agent's review note to Julia Vance conveys the proposed correcting entry - debit account 120000 (Client Cost Advances) / credit account 105000 (IOLTA - Client Trust Account) for $12,460.00 - handed to the close team to action (or similar).
justification: The clean review note must report the action taken on the correction so Julia sees the proposed fix has been worked out and routed to the close team. The correcting entry and its handoff are a distinct content element from the break finding and the lock recommendation.
evidence: See OE 14. Inspec the note/memo content for the proposed correcting entry (DR 120000 / CR 105000, $12,460.00) and a statement that it was handed to the close team to post.

title: The Agent's review note to Julia Vance recommends that the May period (northstar_legal_FP-2026-05) stay unlocked pending the correction posting and independent review, and notes that locking it is Julia Vance's decision (or similar).
justification: The prompt asks Devon to "flag the period still not being locked since that's her call to make and not mine," so the clean review note must carry the lock recommendation. This is a distinct content element from the break finding and the correction handoff.
evidence: See OE 14. Inspect the note/memo content for the recommendation to leave the May period unlocked pending the correction and independent review, and the statement that the lock decision is Julia Vance's call.

title: The Agent does not lock or unlock the May fiscal period (northstar_legal_FP-2026-05).
justification: The prompt states locking the period is Julia's call and not Devon's, and Devon's authority excludes locking periods. Locking or unlocking the period is an authority breach and a task failure.
evidence: See OE 8, OE 14 and the authority guardrails. Confirm there is no lock or unlock action on northstar_legal_FP-2026-05 in the trajectory.

title: The Agent sets a reminder (or schedules a calendar event) to retest the Northstar IOLTA trust tie-out, with a due date later than today (June 12, 2026), for example in late June 2026.
justification: The prompt asks Devon to "set a reminder so we come back and retest the tie-out once the correction has gone through and the month is finally closed." Methodagnostic because a reminder or calendar event both satisfy the ask per the Oracle Events.
evidence: See OE 15. Look for a created reminder or calendar event for the IOLTA retest with a due date/time later than June 12, 2026. Confirm the tool response indicates success.

title: The Agent's retest reminder or calendar event references retesting the Northstar IOLTA trust tie-out (account 105000 versus account 250000) for the May period and notes it is contingent on the correcting entry posting and the May period being closed/locked (or similar wording).
justification: A bare reminder without the contingency does not capture the prompt's "once the correction has gone through and the month is finally closed." The retest subject and its contingency come from the same reminder and are graded together.
evidence: See OE 15. Inspect the reminder title/description for the IOLTA 105000-vs-250000 tie-out retest and the contingency on the correction posting and the May period closing.

title: The Agent reports that the Northstar trust break is $12,460.00, being the difference between the IOLTA client-trust cash in account 105000 ($2,479,288.36) and the client trust liability in account 250000 ($2,466,828.36).
justification: The prompt asks Devon to "pin down what's driving the trust break." The headline number and the two accounts it sits between are the core finding and come from the same balance comparison
evidence: See OE 3, OE 5. Check the final response for the $12,460.00 break between account 105000 ($2,479,288.36) and account 250000 ($2,466,828.36).

title: The Agent identifies the driver of the break as a reclassification entry posted on June 9, 2026 that debited account 105000 (IOLTA - Client Trust Account) and credited account 120000 (Client Cost Advances) for $12,460.00.
justification: The prompt asks the agent to "pin down what's driving the trust break" from the underlying records. The driving entry,  direction, accounts, amount, and post date are the same finding and are graded together.
evidence: See OE 6. Check the final response for a June 9, 2026 reclass debiting 105000 and crediting 120000 for $12,460.00 (entry JE-northstar_legal-FP-2026-05-0055).

title: The Agent states that the reclassification entry was prepared, approved, and posted by the same person, Edith Banda, which is a segregation-of-duties / self-review control failure.
justification: The prompt asks "who is responsible for it, and whether our controls genuinely held." Edith Banda performing all three roles on one entry is the responsibility finding and the control conclusion, from the same entry record.
evidence: See OE 7. Check the final response for Edith Banda named as preparer, approver, and poster of the entry, framed as a self-review / segregation-of-duties breach.

title: The Agent states that the entry was posted into the still-open May period after its lock target with no late-post authorization, a period-close / cutoff control failure (the entry posted June 9, 2026, after the June 3, 2026 lock target).
justification: The prompt flags "some late activity went through after we'd already pulled our interim balances" and asks whether controls held. The unauthorized late post after the cutoff is a distinct control finding from the self-review point.
evidence: See OE 7, OE 8. Check the final response for the entry being posted ater the May lock target while the period was open, with no late-post authorization, framed as a cutoff control failure.

title: The Agent reports that the May period (FP-2026-05) remains unlocked and that locking it is the Audit Partner Julia Vance's responsibility, not Devon's.
justification: The prompt asks the agent to "flag the period still not being locked since that's her call to make and not mine." The open lock status and the lock authority resting with Julia Vance are the same point.
evidence: See OE 8. Check the final response for the May period being open / unlocked and the lock decision belonging to Julia Vance (Audit Partner).

title: The Agent reports that Mia Hartwell could not access the close team's restricted workpaper "Northstar Legal - IOLTA Client Trust Reconciliation Workpaper - May 2026" and that this access has been resolved for her
justification: The prompt notes "when Mia went to check the close team's support she couldn't get to it." Surfacing the access gap and its resolution is a distinct reported finding from the consents check.
evidence: See OE 9, OE 13. Check the final response for Mia's blocked access to the restricted IOLTA workpaper and confirmation that access has been granted.

title: The Agent confirms that the required Northstar client consent letters are in place and active, specifically the Audit-to-Accounting and the Audit-to-Tax information-sharing consents.
justification: The prompt sks the agent to "confirm the client sign-offs we rely on for their files are in place before we lean on anything." Both named consents being active is the same confirmation and graded together.
evidence: See OE 9. Check the final response for confirmation that both the Audit-to-Accounting and Audit-to-Tax Northstar consent letters are active / in place.
